name: React Anti-Patterns
description: Common React anti-patterns to detect and warn about

patterns.name: Array Index as Key
patterns.description: Using array index as key in lists causes bugs when items are reordered, inserted, or deleted.

patterns.name: useEffect Without Dependency Array
patterns.description: useEffect without dependency array runs on EVERY render. Usually a bug.

patterns.name: Copying Props into State
patterns.description: Copying props into state creates a stale data source. Compute derived values directly from props.

patterns.name: Direct DOM Manipulation
patterns.description: Avoid direct DOM manipulation (querySelector, getElementById, innerHTML). Use refs and React state.

patterns.name: dangerouslySetInnerHTML Without Sanitization
patterns.description: Using dangerouslySetInnerHTML without sanitization exposes XSS vulnerabilities.

patterns.name: Deep Prop Drilling
patterns.description: Passing props through 3+ levels of components that don't use them. Use context or composition.

patterns.name: Side Effect in Render Body
patterns.description: Side effects (API calls, subscriptions, mutations) should be in useEffect, not in the render body.

patterns.name: Conditional Hook Call
patterns.description: Hooks must not be called conditionally, inside loops, or after early returns. React relies on call order.

patterns.name: New Object/Array Literal in JSX Props
patterns.description: Creating objects or arrays inline in JSX causes unnecessary re-renders of child components.

patterns.name: Missing Error Boundary
patterns.description: Components that fetch data or render dynamic content should be wrapped in an error boundary.
